functions.name: Govern
functions.description: Cultivate and implement a culture of risk management within organizations designing, developing, deploying, evaluating, or acquiring AI systems. Cross-cutting function that informs and is informed by the other functions.

categories.name: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices are in place and updated
Workforce diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility
Organizational teams are committed to a culture of risk management
Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors
Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks from third parties

functions.name: Map
functions.description: Establish context to frame risks related to an AI system. Identify and categorize risks, impacts, and potential benefits of AI systems.

categories.name: Categorization of the AI system is performed
AI capabilities, targeted usage, goals, and expected benefits and costs are understood
Risks and benefits are mapped for all components of the AI system
Impacts to individuals, groups, communities, organizations, and society are characterized

functions.name: Measure
functions.description: Employ quantitative, qualitative, or mixed-method tools, techniques, and methodologies to analyze, assess, benchmark, and monitor AI risk and related impacts.

categories.name: Appropriate methods and metrics are identified and applied
AI systems are evaluated for trustworthy characteristics
Mechanisms for tracking identified AI risks over time are in place
Feedback about efficacy of measurement is collected and integrated

functions.name: Manage
functions.description: Allocate risk resources to mapped and measured risks on a regular basis and as defined by the Govern function. Manage, prioritize, respond to, and communicate AI risks.

categories.name: AI risks based on assessments and other analytical output are prioritized, responded to, and managed
Strategies to maximize AI benefits and minimize negative impacts are planned, prepared, implemented, documented, and informed by input from relevant AI actors
AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are managed
Risk treatments, including response and recovery, and communication plans are monitored and maintained
